question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user cause onVerified() to cache a copy of the stream file with null bytes and items (line 400-401) via streamFile.copy().clear(), and if copy() is a shallow copy, cause the original streamFile's data to be corrupted before streamFileNotifier.verified() completes, resulting in incorrect records being exported?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file with a consensusEnd value that, when converted via Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, streamFile.getConsensusEnd()) at line 395, overflows or produces an incorrect Instant, causing the cloudStorageLatencyMetric to record a negative or extreme value that masks the fact that records are being delivered out of order?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the endDate check at line 355 to trigger prematurely by supplying a stream file whose dataFilename.getInstant() is after the configured endDate, causing downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false) and permanently stopping all future downloads, resulting in all subsequent records being missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verifySignatures() returns false (line 356) after disabling the downloader, so that the current file is also not exported, creating a gap at the exact endDate boundary in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a FileOperationException or HashMismatchException on every node's data file download (lines 363-372), so that verifySignatures() exhausts all CONSENSUS_REACHED signatures and returns false, causing the stream file to be missing from the mirror node export without any retry mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a TransientProviderException on all node data file downloads (line 363), which is caught and logged as a warning rather than retried, causing the stream file to be permanently missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a generic Exception (line 373) during data file download from one node, which is caught and logged but causes the loop to try the next node, and if all nodes throw generic exceptions, verifySignatures() returns false and the record is missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: setStreamFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user cause setStreamFileIndex() to assign index 0 (line 183) to a non-first stream file by ensuring lastStreamFile is empty and importerProperties.getStartBlockNumber() returns null, causing the stream file index to be incorrect and leading to incorrect record ordering in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: setStreamFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that setStreamFileIndex() uses lastStreamFile.get().map(StreamFile::getIndex).map(v -> v + 1) (lines 178-181) without any gap detection, so that if a stream file is skipped, subsequent files get sequential indices that hide the gap, causing missing records to be undetectable in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user cause streamFileProvider.list() to return signature files in a non-chronological order for some nodes, and since the TreeMultimap uses natural ordering on StreamFilename keys, verify that the ordering is correct and that no records are skipped due to filename comparison anomalies?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user cause signatureFileReader.read() to throw an unchecked exception for a specific node's signature file, which is caught by onErrorContinue at line 221, causing that node's signatures to be absent from the multimap and potentially preventing consensus, leading to missing records in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the listLimit at line 205 to be set to 1 (when downloader is disabled) by triggering a race condition where downloaderProperties.isEnabled() returns false during the call, causing only one signature file to be downloaded and potentially causing records to be missing from the mirror node export?
